title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. meridionale
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. meridionale, also known as the southern oxeye daisy, is a subspecies of daisy native to southern Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. meridionale include reducing inflammation and improving digestive issues, as well as its potential use in treating respiratory problems. Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. meridionale can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with medications.
